summary: A functional test failed because pip could not download a GitHub-hosted test package due to repeated 502 responses from github.com, causing the script to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The test failed because pip was unable to download /pypa/pip-test-package from github.com: requests to https://github.com/.../master.zip hit repeated 502 error responses causing an OSError and non-zero exit.

evidence.reason: The test failure was caused by the tested script exiting with a non-zero code.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows pip could not download the test package from GitHub due to repeated 502 responses (network/server error).

evidence.quote: nox > Command pytest tests/functional --verbose --numprocesses auto --showlocals --durations=15 failed with exit code 1
evidence.reason: Confirms the test session failed overall with exit code 1.

suggested_fix: Re-run the job (transient network/server error). If failures persist, make the test resilient to upstream network issues by mocking the GitHub-hosted package, using a local fixture or cached artifact, or add retry/backoff for downloads; alternatively skip the test when external network access to github.com is unavailable.
